title: Zero-free regions for the Riemann zeta function

thm:1.summary: Asymptotic Vinogradov–Korobov zero-free region: assuming the mean-value bound (1.2) with constant $B$, for large $|t|$ one has $\zeta(\beta+it)\ne 0$ whenever $1-\beta \le \frac{0.05507\, B^{-2/3}}{(\log|t|)^{2/3}(\log\log|t|)^{1/3}}$.

thm:2.summary: Quantitative engine behind Theorem 5: under (1.2) with $0<B<10$, $A\ge 1$ and $T_0 \ge e^{30000}$, no zero $\beta+it$ satisfies $1-\beta \ge \frac{M_1 B^{-2/3}}{(\log t)^{2/3}(\log\log t)^{1/3}}$ with the explicit $M_1$ given. Proved in Section 8.

thm:3.summary: Medium-range engine behind Theorem 4: for $t \ge 5.45\times 10^8$, $\zeta(\beta+it)\ne 0$ for $1-\beta \le \frac{0.04962 - c_6(t)}{J(t)+0.155\log\log t + 0.675}$ with $J(t)=\min(\tfrac14\log t+1.8521,\ \tfrac16\log t+\log\log t+\log 3)$. Proved in Section 9.

thm:4.summary: Explicit classical-type zero-free region: $\zeta(\beta+it)\ne 0$ for $|t|\ge 3$ and $1-\beta \le \frac{1}{8.464\,\log|t|}$.

thm:5.summary: The headline explicit Vinogradov–Korobov zero-free region: $\zeta(\beta+it)\ne 0$ in $1-\beta \le \frac{1}{57.54\,(\log|t|)^{2/3}(\log\log|t|)^{1/3}}$ for $|t|\ge 3$ — the strongest fully-explicit VK region of the paper.

lem:2.1.summary: Hadamard-factorization representation: for $f$ a quotient of entire functions of order $<k$ with $f(0)\ne 0$, both $f'(z)/f(z)$ and $\log|f(z)|$ are expressed as sums over zeros/poles $\rho$ with $|\rho|\le 2|z|$ plus an $O_f(|z|^{k-1})$ (resp. $O_f(|z|^k)$) error.

lem:2.2.summary: The 'zero detector' identity: $-\Re\frac{f'(z_0)}{f(z_0)}$ equals a localized cotangent sum $\frac{\pi}{2\eta}\sum_{|\Re(z_0-\rho)|\le\eta} m_\rho \Re\cot(\frac{\pi(\rho-z_0)}{2\eta})$ plus a $\cosh^{-2}$-weighted integral of $\log|f|$ on two vertical lines, for almost all $\eta>0$.

lem:3.1.summary: Basic $\zeta$ bounds: for $\sigma>1$, $\frac{1}{\zeta(\sigma)} \le |\zeta(\sigma+it)| \le \zeta(\sigma)$ and $|-\frac{\zeta'}{\zeta}(\sigma+it)| < \frac{1}{\sigma-1}$; and for $1<\sigma\le 1.8$, $\zeta(\sigma) \le 0.64 + \frac{1}{\sigma-1}$.

lem:3.2.summary: Bound on the logarithmic derivative on the line $\Re s = -\tfrac12$: $\left|\frac{\zeta'(-\frac12+iu)}{\zeta(-\frac12+iu)}\right| \le 4.62 + \tfrac12\log(1+u^2/9)$.

lem:3.4.summary: Converts a $\zeta$ growth bound $|\zeta(\sigma+iy)| \le X t^Y (\log t)^Z$ (with $Y+Z\ge 0.1$) on a fixed line into an explicit integral bound used to detect zeros; the bridge from exponential-sum input to the zero count.

lem:4.1.summary: Master zero-detection inequality: under (1.2), for $s=\sigma+it$ with suitable $\eta,\sigma,t$, $-\Re\frac{\zeta'(s)}{\zeta(s)}$ is bounded above by a localized sum over zeros $\rho$ in a strip plus controlled error — the analytic core that turns proximity of a zero into a contradiction.

lem:4.2.summary: Zero-counting bound: under (1.2), for $0<R\le 1/4$, $t\ge 100$, $N(t,R) \le 1.3478 R^{3/2} B\log t + 3.752 + \frac{\log A - \log R + \frac23\log\log t}{1.879}$.

lem:4.3.summary: Bound on the sum $\sum_{|1+it-\rho|\ge v} \frac{1}{|1+it-\rho|^2}$ over zeros near $1+it$, in terms of $\log t$, $\log A$, $\log\log t$ and the zero count $N(t,v)$.

lem:4.4.summary: Elementary cotangent inequality: for $\Re z \ge 0$ and $|z|\le \pi/2$, $\Re\cot z - \frac{1}{z} + \frac{4z}{\pi^2} \ge 0$.

lem:4.5.summary: Laplace-transform lemma: for non-negative $f$ with absolutely convergent transform $F(z)=\int_0^\infty f(y)e^{-zy}dy$ and $F_0(z)=F(z)-f(0)/z$ satisfying $|F_0(z)| \le D/|z|^2$, controls the relevant contour contributions.

lem:4.6.summary: The $\Re K(s)$ bound: for $s=1+it$, $t\ge 1000$, $0<\eta\le\tfrac12$ and a compactly-supported $f$, bounds $\Re K(s)$ by a localized sum over zeros within $\eta$ of $1+it$; the assembled inequality fed into the proof of Theorem 2.

lem:5.1.summary: Trigonometric / positivity inequality: for coefficients $b_0,\dots,b_4$ from (5.2), $\int_{-\infty}^\infty \frac{1}{\cosh^2 u}\sum_{j=1}^4 b_j \log|\zeta(1+\eta+ijt_1+iut_2)|\,du \ge -2b_0\log\zeta(1+\eta)$ — the positivity input replacing the classical $3+4\cos\theta+\cos2\theta$.

lem:7.1.summary: Inequality for the real part of a zero: combines the zero-detection and positivity inputs into a lower bound on $1-\beta$ for any putative zero $\beta+it$, the step the explicit constants are optimized over.

lem:9.1.summary: Medium-range technical lemma supporting the proof of Theorem 3 for $t \ge 5.45\times 10^8$.

lem:9.2.summary: Second medium-range technical lemma feeding the proof of Theorem 3.

edges.note: The explicit VK region (Theorem 5) is the $T_0\to 3$ form of the quantitative Theorem 2, proved in Section 8.
The explicit classical-type region (Theorem 4) follows from the medium-range Theorem 3, proved in Section 9.
The asymptotic conditional VK region rests on the master zero-detection inequality (4.1).
The proof of Theorem 2 (Section 8) assembles the $\Re K(s)$ bound of Lemma 4.6.
Theorem 2 uses the master zero-detection inequality (4.1).
Theorem 2 uses the trigonometric/positivity inequality of Section 5 (Lemma 5.1).
Theorem 2 uses the inequality for the real part of a zero (Section 7).
Section 9's proof of Theorem 3 uses Lemma 9.1.
Section 9's proof of Theorem 3 uses Lemma 9.2.
The medium-range argument uses the growth-to-integral bound of Lemma 3.4.
The zero-detection inequality is built on the cotangent-integral 'zero detector' identity of Lemma 2.2.
Both represent $f'/f$ and $\log|f|$ via the Hadamard factorization; 2.2 localizes the 2.1 representation into the detector identity.
The $\Re K(s)$ bound uses the Laplace-transform lemma 4.5.
The $\Re K(s)$ bound uses the elementary cotangent inequality (4.4).
The $K(s)$ construction reuses the localized zero-sum mechanism of the detection inequality (4.1).
The sum-over-zeros bound uses the zero count $N(t,R)$ from Lemma 4.2.
Uses the global zero-sum bound $\sum_\rho 1/|\rho|^2 \le 0.0463$ (Lemma 3.3).
The zero count is derived from the growth-to-integral bound of Lemma 3.4.
The detection inequality uses the basic $\zeta$ and $\zeta'/\zeta$ bounds of Lemma 3.1.
The integral bound uses the logarithmic-derivative bound on $\Re s=-\tfrac12$ (Lemma 3.2).

main_results.description: The zero-free regions: the asymptotic conditional VK region (Thm 1) and the two fully-explicit regions — VK-type (Thm 5) and classical-type (Thm 4).

quantitative_engines.description: Quantitative theorems with explicit ranges and constants that the explicit regions are read off from.

zero_detector.description: Hadamard-factorization representation of $f'/f$ and $\log|f|$, localized into the cotangent-integral 'zero detector' identity.

zeta_bounds.description: Explicit bounds on $\zeta$, $\zeta'/\zeta$, the global zero-sum, and the growth-to-integral bridge from exponential-sum input.

zero_detection.description: The master detection inequality and its supporting zero-counting, sum-over-zeros, cotangent, Laplace-transform and $\Re K(s)$ bounds.

positivity_and_realpart.description: The trigonometric positivity inequality replacing the classical $3+4\cos\theta+\cos2\theta$, and the resulting inequality for the real part of a zero where constants are optimized.

medium_range.description: Technical lemmas specific to the medium-range Theorem 3 ($t \ge 5.45\times 10^8$).
